security_audit.summary: This is a legitimate document processing skill for creating, editing, and analyzing DOCX files. Static analysis flagged patterns in documentation (example commands), XML schemas (namespace URLs), and validation scripts (file operations). All findings are false positives - no malicious code execution, data exfiltration, or unauthorized access patterns detected. The skill provides safe document manipulation capabilities using standard Python and JavaScript libraries.

low_findings.title: Documentation Example Commands Flagged as External Commands
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged shell commands in markdown documentation (e.g., pandoc, python scripts) as external command execution. These are documentation examples showing CLI usage, not actual code execution by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: These are clearly documentation examples in markdown code blocks showing CLI usage. No user input is being passed to these commands in the skill itself.

low_findings.title: XML Schema Namespace URLs Flagged as Network Requests
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged XML namespace URLs in XSD schema files as hardcoded URLs/network access. These are standard XML schema definitions, not network requests.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: These are XML namespace declarations (e.g., xmlns:xsd='http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema') - standard schema references, not actual network calls.

low_findings.title: Document Processing Scripts Flagged as Filesystem Operations
low_findings.description: Python scripts for packing/unpacking OOXML documents were flagged for filesystem operations. This is the legitimate purpose of the skill - document file manipulation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: These scripts are for legitimate document processing - unpacking DOCX (which is a ZIP) to access XML contents, and repacking. Expected behavior for a document manipulation skill.

low_findings.title: XML Schema Element Names Flagged as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged XML element names containing 'md5' as weak cryptographic algorithms. These are schema element names, not actual cryptographic code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: These are XML element/attribute names in schemas (e.g., 'md5Hash' as an element name), not actual cryptographic implementation code.

low_findings.title: XML Schema Elements Flagged as C2 Keywords
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged XML element names containing words like 'execute', 'script', 'run' as C2 keywords. These are schema definitions for document processing, not command-and-control code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: These are XML elements for document features (e.g., 'execEvt' for execution events in Office schemas), not malicious C2 infrastructure.

content.user_title: Create and Edit Professional DOCX Documents
content.value_statement: Users need to create, edit, and analyze Microsoft Word documents but lack knowledge of the complex OOXML format. This skill provides Claude with the tools and workflows to manipulate DOCX files using Python and JavaScript libraries, including support for tracked changes, comments, and formatting preservation.
actual_capabilities: Create new DOCX documents using JavaScript (docx-js library) or Python (Document library)
Edit existing DOCX files with tracked changes, comments, and formatting preservation
Extract text and metadata from DOCX files using pandoc or raw XML parsing
Analyze document structure including comments, tracked changes, and embedded media
Pack and unpack OOXML documents for direct XML manipulation
Perform redlining workflows for legal, academic, and business document reviews
limitations: Cannot edit encrypted or password-protected documents without the password
Complex formatting may require direct XML manipulation rather than high-level APIs
Does not support older binary DOC format (pre-OOXML Word documents)
Some advanced Word features like VBA macros cannot be created or modified

use_cases.title: Generate Business Documents
use_cases.description: Create professional Word documents with formatting, tables, headers, and images for business reports, contracts, or proposals.

use_cases.title: Review and Edit Legal Documents
use_cases.description: Use redlining workflow to review legal contracts or academic papers with tracked changes while preserving original formatting.

use_cases.title: Extract Document Content
use_cases.description: Extract text, metadata, comments, and embedded media from existing DOCX files for analysis or migration.

prompt_templates.title: Create Simple Document
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a DOCX document with a title heading, a paragraph of introduction text, and a bulleted list with 3 items. Use center alignment for the title.

prompt_templates.title: Edit Existing Document
prompt_templates.prompt: Edit the existing document at [filepath] by adding a new paragraph after the second heading. The new paragraph should say: 'This document was updated on [date]'.

prompt_templates.title: Extract Text Content
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract all text content from [filepath.docx] and output it as plain text. Preserve paragraph breaks.

prompt_templates.title: Track Changes in Document
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the redlining workflow to modify [filepath.docx]: change all instances of 'company name' to 'Acme Corp', and add a comment explaining the change. Save the result as [output.docx].

output_examples.input: Create a DOCX document with a title heading 'Quarterly Report' and a table with 3 columns and 2 rows
output_examples.output: Successfully created quarterly-report.docx with a centered title heading and a 3x2 table containing empty cells ready for data entry
Found 5 comments in the document: [Comment 1: 'Verify this clause with legal', Comment 2: 'Update effective date', ...]

output_examples.input: Add tracked changes to document.docx to replace '30 days' with '60 days' in the payment terms section
output_examples.output: Tracked change added: deleted '30 days' and inserted '60 days' in the payment terms section. Change is visible in Word with revision tracking enabled.

best_practices: Always read the full docx-js.md and ooxml.md documentation files before creating or editing documents
Use the redlining workflow for editing documents you did not create, especially for legal or business documents
Test document output in Microsoft Word after creation to verify formatting renders correctly
anti_patterns: Using newline characters (\n) in TextRun elements instead of separate Paragraph elements
Attempting to edit complex formatting without reading the full documentation first
Modifying third-party documents without using the redlining workflow, which loses change history

faq.question: What libraries does this skill use?
faq.answer: This skill uses the docx-js library for JavaScript/TypeScript document creation and the Python Document library (python-docx) for editing. It also uses pandoc for text extraction.

faq.question: Can I edit password-protected documents?
faq.answer: No, password-protected or encrypted DOCX files cannot be edited without the password. You will need to ask the user for the password.

faq.question: What is the redlining workflow?
faq.answer: Redlining is a document review process that uses tracked changes to show all modifications. It is required for legal, academic, business, and government documents to maintain audit trails.

faq.question: Can I create documents with images?
faq.answer: Yes, both the JavaScript and Python libraries support embedding images in documents. Refer to the documentation files for specific syntax.

faq.question: Does this support older DOC files?
faq.answer: No, this skill only supports the OOXML format (DOCX files). Older binary DOC files would need to be converted to DOCX first using pandoc or Microsoft Word.

faq.question: How do I extract comments from a document?
faq.answer: Extract comments by unpacking the DOCX file (which is a ZIP archive) and reading the word/comments.xml file, or by using the Document library's comment handling methods.
